title: Pricing table: hardcoded model strings drift from live rates, inference via substring match is fragile
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

Dimension: Architectural Alignment (2) / SOLID/OCP (3.2)

Location: src/session/pricing.rs:20-131 (get_pricing).

Violation: A 17-branch if/else-if chain (cyclomatic complexity ~18, exceeds the 15 threshold from 3.5) doing substring matches on model names to look up prices.

1. OCP violation: adding a new model requires editing a central function. SRP violation: pricing data and pricing-selection logic are fused.
2. Ordering hazards: 'gpt-5.2' branch at line 38 must come BEFORE 'gpt-5' branch at line 53 to avoid shadowing. 'gemini-2...flash' at 103 must come before 'gemini-2' at 108. Any reorder breaks pricing silently.
3. Drift: there is no source-of-truth for these numbers. Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, DeepSeek, Qwen all update prices independently; this file will be wrong in 3 months. calculate_cost feeds directly into user-facing cost displays and budget limits — wrong pricing means wrong budget enforcement.
4. Cache-write multiplier hardcoded at 1.25x (line 148) — Anthropic cache writes are 1.25x input for ephemeral and 2x for 1hr caches. The code assumes ephemeral, so 1-hour cache writes are UNDER-billed by 60 percent.
5. calculate_cost returns None for unknown models — which masks as 'free' in downstream code that does .unwrap_or(0.0).

Mandated Refactor:
1. Replace the if-chain with a table: static PRICING_TABLE: &[(prefix_pattern, ModelPricing)]. Iterate and return the first match. Makes ordering explicit and testable.
2. Load the table from a versioned YAML file (alongside existing config/mcp configs), with the struct literal as the embedded fallback. Lets users update pricing without a recompile.
3. Split cache_write_multiplier into ephemeral_5m and ephemeral_1hr fields. Pick based on the request's cache_control TTL.
4. calculate_cost returns Result<f64, PricingError::UnknownModel(String)> — force caller to handle missing pricing explicitly, not swallow as zero.
5. Add a property test: for every registered model name across providers.rs and config.rs, pricing must resolve.
